authoritative_sources.title: Use the space intake portal to submit your requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA is the authoritative federal real-estate provider for public buildings and leased federal space. This page defines the formal intake workflow, work-pattern capture, CPA creation, and the separation between intake, release, and reimbursable work paths.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Demand intake and planning package creation
used_for: separation of planning intake from release or execution workflows

authoritative_sources.title: Occupancy Agreement Space Inventory System (OASIS)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA publishes the official occupancy agreement and space inventory workflow for its customers. The page establishes OASIS as the system of record for OA review, approval workflow, space inventory, and change handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Occupancy and utilization reporting guidelines
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official GSA guidance defines which spaces must be reported, what identifiers and square-footage metrics govern reporting, and the current 150 USF per person design requirement for office space planning baselines.

authoritative_sources.title: Internal Space Allocation, Design, and Management Policy
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This active GSA directive is an official policy source for workplace design, internal space allocation, occupancy measurement, utilization reporting, portfolio optimization, and headquarters consolidation planning.

authoritative_sources.title: Obtaining a Permit
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: A city building department is the authoritative local permitting regulator. This guidance defines permit filing roles, design-professional involvement, and permit prerequisites that a space-planning support lane cannot bypass or reinterpret.
used_for: refusal of permit-bypass or code-interpretation requests

authoritative_sources.title: Residential Building Final Inspection Quick Sheet
authoritative_sources.publisher: Seattle Department of Construction and Inspections
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official final-inspection quick sheet provides a concrete public baseline for approved plans on site, completed associated permits, special inspection letters, and test results required before final inspection and completion.

authoritative_sources.title: Safety and Health Regulations for Construction
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the primary U.S. workplace-safety regulator. Part 1926 is the controlling federal baseline for construction and renovation safety duties, hazard controls, and stop conditions that planning cannot override.
used_for: refusal of safety-signoff or execution authority

authoritative_sources.title: Commissioning Documents
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: WBDG is a recognized public building guidance platform. This commissioning reference details owners project requirements, basis of design, systems manuals, issues logs, training records, and final commissioning reports that define closeout and handoff evidence for space changes affecting building systems.

workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and request normalization
workflow_stages.description: Classify incoming space requests, normalize business demand, identify the current space record, and confirm whether the intake packet is complete enough for planning review.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess occupancy, utilization, usable square footage, adjacency, privacy, support-space ratios, swing-space capacity, and phasing constraints before recommending allocations or priorities.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Site-readiness, permit, and inspection gating
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether a space plan can move toward implementation based on permit filing status, approved plans, site access, work-order readiness, safety posture, and inspection prerequisites.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closeout, occupancy release, and escalation packaging
workflow_stages.description: Review inspection, commissioning, training, and closeout evidence, then escalate unresolved gaps or authority conflicts instead of presenting a final occupancy or implementation approval.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

artifacts: Space intake requests and CPA-style requirements packages
Occupancy agreements, OA numbers, RPUIDs, and space inventory records
Occupancy and utilization reports with USF and seat counts
Floor plans, stacking diagrams, adjacency matrices, and blocking plans
Work orders, move phasing plans, and project schedules
Permits, approved plans, and certificate-of-occupancy dependencies
Inspection records, special inspection letters, and test reports
Site logs, safety observations, systems manuals, training records, and closeout packages
systems_of_record: Inspection and special inspection record systems

allowed: Assess whether space demand, utilization evidence, and public-baseline prerequisites support a bounded allocation or phasing recommendation.
Identify occupancy, adjacency, swing-space, permit, inspection, and closeout blockers for transparent planning and prioritization support.
Prepare evidence-backed recommendation packages, constraint logs, and escalation notes without claiming final approval authority.
escalate: Permit, inspection, safety, or closeout conditions that materially change whether a restack, move, or occupancy plan can proceed.
Conflicting tenant policy and public baseline on utilization standards, occupancy release, permit handling, or completion evidence.
Requests that shift into project controls, permitting, inspection, safety, move-management, workplace-strategy, licensed-trade, or design-authority ownership.
refuse: Final permit approval, code interpretation, architect or engineer judgment, or licensed trade signoff.
Final budget, lease, staffing, schedule-commitment, or operational approval.
Any request to bypass site-safety, inspection, accessibility, or closeout evidence requirements.

domain_failure_modes: space is allocated or moved without confirmed OA context, occupancy metrics, or record-of-truth floor plans
headcount and support-space demand are treated as feasible even though swing space, accessibility, or adjacency constraints are not validated
restack or churn work is advanced without permit status, approved drawings, access controls, or work-order readiness
inspection, commissioning, or closeout evidence is ignored and occupancy is treated as ready anyway
schedule pressure overrides new site-safety observations or failed inspection outcomes
the lane is pressured into code interpretation, licensed design judgment, permit approval, budget approval, or final space assignment authority
tenant-specific neighborhood standards, privacy requirements, or local AHJ rules differ from the public baseline and the lane guesses instead of escalating

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant
Tradeoffs require approval outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public GSA allocation and utilization baselines do not determine each tenant's approved neighborhood mix, privacy exceptions, or specialty support-space ratios. Runtime must retrieve tenant policy instead of guessing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific permit and occupancy release rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public permit and inspection examples demonstrate boundary behavior, but the exact permit set, accessibility checks, and occupancy release conditions vary by locality and building type. Runtime must retrieve the governing jurisdiction and AHJ records before proceeding.
